title: Chemical & New Methods of Sterilization MCQ – ORT Textbook
source: Textbook for Operation Room Technicians – Ch 78 Chemical Methods & Ch 79 New Methods of Sterilization (Pages 369–374)

questions.question: Ethylene Oxide (EO) gas is effective in killing which of the following?

options.A: All types of bacteria, viruses and spores

questions.explanation: Page 369: Ethylene Oxide is a very poisonous gas which kills all types of bacteria, viruses and spores.

questions.question: In what form is Ethylene Oxide gas available in cylinders, tanks or cartridges?
questions.explanation: Page 369: EO is available in cylinder, tank or cartridges in the form of liquid.

questions.question: Which are the two methods of sterilization with EO gas?

options.A: The gas chamber method and the micro dose method

questions.explanation: Page 369: Sterilization with EO gas can be done by two means – the gas chamber method and the micro dose method.

questions.question: When EO gas is used with gas diffusion or bag sterilization for safety, which gas is used as a diluent?
questions.explanation: Page 369: It is used with gas diffusion or bag sterilization for safety where other gases are used as diluents such as CO2.

questions.question: How should the room housing the EO sterilizer be designed?

options.B: A small room on positive pressure
options.C: A separate and big room on negative pressure, vented outside the building
options.D: A sealed room with no ventilation

questions.explanation: Page 369: The sterilizer should be installed in a separate and big room which is on negative pressure, with the relief valve vented outside the building.

questions.question: What type of items are generally sterilized with EO gas?

options.B: Items which cannot stand high temperature

questions.explanation: Page 369: Types of items sterilized with EO gas are those items which cannot stand high temperature.

questions.question: At what temperature is the item generally kept during EO sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 369: Generally the item is kept at 55°C (range 37°C to 60°C) for 105 to 300 minutes.

questions.question: What humidity range is required for EO gas sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 369: The humidity should be 45% to 75% during EO sterilization.

questions.question: What quantity of EO gas should be used during the sterilization process?
questions.explanation: Page 369: Quantity of gas should be 800 mg to 1200 mg/litre during the sterilization process with EO gas.

questions.question: Why should the sterilizer door be opened immediately after EO sterilization is over?

options.D: Because at that time the density is low

questions.explanation: Page 369: The door should be immediately opened as soon as sterilization is over because at that time the density is low.

questions.question: Which property makes EO gas hazardous to handle?
questions.explanation: Page 369: EO gas is inflammable and explosive, and is very soluble in packets, rubber and plastic.

questions.question: Which of the following items can be sterilized with EO gas?

options.C: Endoscope, cystoscope, laparoscope and cardiac catheter

questions.explanation: Page 369: EO gas sterilizes endoscope, cystoscope, laparoscope, cardiac catheter, parts of heart lung machine, pace maker, breast implant, nebulizer, parts of ventilator, blankets and mattress.

questions.question: What should be available for staff during the use of EO because it is a poisonous gas?
questions.explanation: Page 370: EO is a poisonous gas and equipment for breathing should be available during its use.

questions.question: Which complication can occur in staff if EO gas is not completely removed?

options.A: Skin rashes and oedema of the laryngo-tracheal part causing breathing difficulties

questions.explanation: Page 370: If gas is not completely removed, complications include skin rashes, oedema of the laryngo-tracheal part causing breathing difficulties.

questions.question: What can happen to blood cells due to excessive levels of EO gas?
questions.explanation: Page 370: Blood cells can be destroyed due to excessive levels of EO gas.

questions.question: Approximately how long does the complete EO sterilization process take, making it expensive?
questions.explanation: Page 370: The sterilization process is prolonged and at least 12 hours are required for complete process, so a large stock of items is needed, making it expensive to maintain a big inventory.

questions.question: What type of packing paper is required for items to be sterilized with EO gas?

options.C: A packet with plastic on one side and paper on the other that allows passage of air and steam

questions.explanation: Page 370: Special packing paper is required – the packet should have plastic on one side and paper on the other side which should allow the passage of air and steam.

questions.question: Which biological test organism is best for testing EO gas sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 370: A biological test should be done every week and Bacillus subtilis is best for testing EO gas.

questions.question: For how long should the record of EO sterilization be maintained?
questions.explanation: Page 370: Record of sterilization should be maintained for two years.

questions.question: How often should the biological test be done for EO gas monitoring?
questions.explanation: Page 370: Biological test should be done every week for EO gas monitoring; chemical tape is used daily.

questions.question: What kind of sterilizer is needed for formaldehyde sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 370: Sterilizer for formaldehyde – a special pre-vacuum sterilizer is needed for this.

questions.question: At what temperature and time are items disinfected with formaldehyde?
questions.explanation: Page 370: With formaldehyde, items are kept at the temperature of 71°C to 77°C for 33 minutes to disinfect them.

questions.question: Why are items less damaged when sterilized with formaldehyde?

options.A: Because of the high pressure used
options.D: Because the process is very fast

questions.explanation: Page 370: With formaldehyde the things are less damaged due to the low temperature.

questions.question: Why is formaldehyde gas generally NOT used in hospitals?
questions.explanation: Page 371: Hospital use of formaldehyde – this gas being poisonous is not used in the hospitals.

questions.question: What is formalin liquid used for?
questions.explanation: Page 371: Formalin liquid is used for storing the surgical specimen, while formalin tablets are used to sterilize endoscopes and microscopes.

questions.question: Which chemical is described as the commonly used chemical nowadays for sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 371: Glutaraldehyde 2% is the commonly used chemical nowadays.

questions.question: For the first time, how long are instruments soaked in glutaraldehyde?
questions.explanation: Page 371: First time the instruments are soaked overnight and subsequently for 3-4 hours before use in glutaraldehyde.

questions.question: Why should instruments be washed well with soap and water before dipping in the chemical?

options.C: So that stains are removed, which is essential for the chemical to act

questions.explanation: Page 371: Before dipping in the chemical, instruments should be washed well with soap and water using a brush so that stains are removed. This is essential for the chemical to act.

questions.question: Glutaraldehyde should be known as which of the following?

options.A: A high class disinfectant rather than a sterilent

questions.explanation: Page 371: It should be known as a high class disinfectant rather than a Sterilent.

questions.question: How does water on instruments affect the liquid chemical's sterilization capability?

options.C: It dilutes the concentration, decreasing sterilization capabilities

questions.explanation: Page 371: The water on instruments dilutes the concentration of the chemical, which decreases the sterilization capabilities.

questions.question: For how long is a prepared solution of glutaraldehyde chemical effective?
questions.explanation: Page 371: A solution of chemical prepared once is effective for 14 to 28 days.

questions.question: What is the name of one popular preparation of glutaraldehyde, and at what concentration is it used?
questions.explanation: Page 371: One popular preparation of glutaraldehyde is Cidex. This is used as 0.55%.

questions.question: Which chemical is described as being better than 2% Glutaraldehyde but more expensive?
questions.explanation: Page 371: Ortho Phthalaldehyde – this chemical is better than 2% Glutaraldehyde, but it is expensive.

questions.question: What is a noted disadvantage of Ortho Phthalaldehyde on the skin?

options.A: It has no effect on skin

questions.explanation: Page 371: With Ortho Phthalaldehyde, the skin is stained gray, and it can cause allergy.

questions.question: According to the text, how long does glutaraldehyde take to kill all organisms?
questions.explanation: Page 371: Glutaraldehyde takes 22 hours to kill all organisms, and formaldehyde takes even longer time.

questions.question: What is one of the main reasons new methods of sterilization are constantly being developed?

options.C: To sterilize endoscopes due to the increase in minimal invasive procedures
options.D: To increase the use of CFCs

questions.explanation: Page 372: New methods are required to sterilize endoscopes as there is an increase in the number of minimal invasive procedures, and there is a need to eliminate CFCs.

questions.question: In plasma sterilization, plasma is produced when a chemical such as hydrogen peroxide is introduced into what?

options.D: A strong electric or magnetic field

questions.explanation: Page 372: Plasma can be produced when a chemical such as hydrogen peroxide is introduced into a strong electric or magnetic field and excited to a state capable of disrupting the cell membrane and nucleic acid of microorganisms.

questions.question: The sterilization cycle of hydrogen peroxide plasma is made of which two phases?
questions.explanation: Page 372: The sterilization cycle of hydrogen peroxide plasma is made of 2 phases – the diffusion phase and the plasma phase.

questions.question: In the plasma phase, how much radio frequency power is applied to the chamber?
questions.explanation: Page 372: In the plasma phase, 400 watts of radio frequency is applied to the chamber, making the H2O2 a plasma made of hydro peroxyl radicals.

questions.question: Why is paper NOT used in packing for plasma sterilization?

options.B: Because it absorbs excess hydrogen peroxide

questions.explanation: Page 373: Paper is not used in plasma sterilization packing as it absorbs excess hydrogen peroxide; items are covered with double layers of polypropylene.

questions.question: Which company introduced STERRAD 100 & STERRAD 50 for plasma sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 373: Johnson and Johnson company has introduced STERRAD 100 & STERRAD 50 for plasma sterilization; the latter is smaller and consists of one shelf.

questions.question: Which biological test organism is used for plasma sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 373: Biological test for plasma sterilization is done with bacillus atrophies (Bacillus atrophaeus).

questions.question: Why does plasma sterilization NOT require aeration?

options.A: Because the end products are oxygen and water
options.B: Because it leaves toxic residue that evaporates
options.C: Because it uses very high temperature
options.D: Because the items stay sealed forever

questions.explanation: Page 373: There is no need for aeration as the end products of plasma sterilization are oxygen and water, which are not toxic.

questions.question: Per-acetic acid combines acetic acid, hydrogen peroxide and water in what form?
questions.explanation: Page 373: Per-acetic acid combines acetic acid, hydrogen peroxide and water in a non-corrosive form.

questions.question: For how long and at what temperature are items soaked in per-acetic acid?
questions.explanation: Page 373: Soak the items for 12 minutes at a temperature of 122°F–131°F in per-acetic acid; it has a quick processing time of approximately 30 minutes.

questions.question: How many times can a per-acetic acid solution be used?
questions.explanation: Page 374: With per-acetic acid, the solution can be used only once; it is corrosive to certain items and an irritant.

questions.question: What is the cycle time and temperature for ozone sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 374: Ozone cycle time is 4-5 hours with a temperature of 85°F–94°F; 6-12% concentration is continuously pumped through the chamber.

questions.question: Ozone is an efficient sterilent capable of destroying many pathogens including which of the following?
questions.explanation: Page 374: Ozone is an efficient sterilent because of strong oxidizing properties capable of destroying many pathogens including prions. The health limit for ozone is 5 ppm.

questions.question: Nitrogen dioxide (NO2) is also known by which other name?
questions.explanation: Page 374: Nitrogen dioxide (NO2) is also known as dinitrogen tetroxide (N2O4). It has a boiling point of 21°C at sea level and is used in the form of liquid.

questions.question: Which biological indicator is used for nitrogen dioxide (NO2) sterilization?
questions.explanation: Page 374: Geobacillus stearothermophilus is the biological indicator for nitrogen dioxide (NO2) sterilization. NO2 is less corrosive and requires no aeration.
